T1.title: rename two control buttons
T1.desc: Rename two control buttons in the control panel: the button currently labelled `Reset` should read `Restart`, and the button labelled `Pause` should read `Play / Pause`. Do not change their behavior.
T1.accept: The control panel shows buttons labelled `Restart` and `Play / Pause`. The project must pass `npm run typecheck`. No other behavior changes.

T2.title: change default gravity + clamp the slider
T2.desc: Two changes to gravity: (1) change the default gravity value from 980 to 500; (2) make the Gravity slider clamp the value it applies into the range [0, 2000].
T2.accept: On load, settings.gravity is 500. The Gravity slider clamps its applied value to [0, 2000]. The project must pass `npm run typecheck`.

T3.title: add air drag
T3.desc: Add air drag (air resistance). Requirements: (1) add a numeric field `drag` to the live settings in src/state/settings.ts (default 0, intended range 0..2); (2) implement a drag acceleration that opposes a body's velocity (contribution to acceleration = -settings.drag * body.vel) and include it in the per-step total acceleration; (3) add a slider labelled `Drag` (min 0, max 2, step 0.05) to the control panel that updates settings.drag.
T3.accept: settings.drag exists (default 0). With gravity 0 and drag > 0, bodies lose kinetic energy faster than with drag = 0. A Drag slider is present. The project must pass `npm run typecheck`.

T4.title: make Vector2 immutable
T4.desc: Refactor the Vector2 class to be immutable: the methods add, sub, scale, and normalize must each return a NEW Vector2 and must NOT mutate `this`. Update every call site across the codebase so that the simulation's behavior is exactly unchanged. (clone, set, dot, length, lengthSq may stay.)
T4.accept: Vector2.add/sub/scale/normalize return new instances and do not mutate this. The simulation behaves identically to before (same motion). The project must pass `npm run typecheck`.

T5.title: make the simulation conserve energy
T5.desc: Make the simulation conserve energy. A moving ball should keep its kinetic energy over time: collisions should be perfectly elastic and nothing should damp or add friction to motion. Adjust the relevant constants/parameters wherever they live in the code.
T5.accept: With gravity 0, total kinetic energy stays essentially constant over thousands of steps (no decay). The project must pass `npm run typecheck`.
